segments.text: A Kubernetes node is the machine that runs your workloads.
Every node hosts three components: the kubelet, the container runtime, and kube-proxy.
The kubelet is the agent on each node.
It registers the node with the control plane and runs the Pods assigned to that node.
Nodes can register themselves — the default — or you can add them manually.
The control plane tracks node health through conditions.
Ready means the node is healthy and can schedule Pods.
MemoryPressure, DiskPressure, and PIDPressure are warning signals.
NetworkUnavailable means the network isn't configured.
When a node goes unhealthy, the node controller evicts its Pods.
Rate limits prevent cascading failures across the cluster.
Nodes are the foundation. Everything else runs on top of them.
